name: TypeScript Stack
description: Opinionated rules for TypeScript projects. Enforces strict mode, co-located tests, explicit types, and consistent import patterns across Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot.
author: Instill AI

preferences.rule: Always use TypeScript strict mode. Never use `any` unless explicitly justified with a comment.
Co-locate tests with source files: `foo.ts` → `foo.test.ts` in the same directory.
Prefer named exports over default exports. Default exports make refactoring harder.
Use `zod` for runtime validation of external data (API responses, env vars, form inputs). Never trust untyped external data.
Prefer `const` over `let`. Use `let` only when reassignment is unavoidable.
Write explicit return types on all exported functions. Inferred return types are fine for internal helpers.
Use `structuredClone` instead of JSON.parse/stringify for deep cloning objects.
Before adding a new dependency, check if the stdlib or an existing package already covers the need.
